element_introduction_order.mastery_context: map revelation and object interaction; combines with charge for distance attacks
resource management constraint defining level pacing
temporary offensive enhancement for specific stages

element_introduction_order.element: Metasphere transformations (seagull, jellyfish, shark, fish school, Vortex drone)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: level-specific mechanic altering movement and interaction
precision piloting; failure forces stage restart
giant enemy archetype requiring tactical ramming
hostile alien combat and ambush mechanic
branching timeline exploration (bright future vs. dark future)
artificial gravity and water-tube navigation in hostile setting

typical_tension_triggers: low air supply approaching surface requirement
enemy ambush (Vortex drones, giant moray eels)
pseudo-3D ring-navigation sequences with restart penalty
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Breath-gated tempo with narrative-driven intensity escalation. Early game establishes air-surfacing rhythm as primary pacing constraint. Mid-game introduces puzzle and transformation variety, maintaining moderate tension. Late game accelerates through time-travel sequences, environmental hazards, and combat intensity. Final stages maintain high tension through boss encounters and story climax.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Underwater caves and side passages may contain optional globes or power-ups; exploration encouraged by sonar echolocation mechanic
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Off-path exploration and thorough sonar use reveals hidden objects and alternate routes; globe collection invites discovery-based gameplay

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introductory ramming and sonar mechanics with low enemy density; air-surfacing establishes foundational rhythm constraint
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Puzzle complexity increases (glyphs, mazes, transformations); enemy encounters become more frequent; pseudo-3D sequences introduce restart-penalty stakes
difficulty_curve.late_game: Vortex combat and dark-future navigation demand precision; globe-holder and queen battles require mastery of all accumulated mechanics; time-travel narrative raises thematic stakes
notable_spikes: First pseudo-3D ring-navigation sequence (steep learning curve for new mechanic)
Lunar Bay ambush sequence (sudden combat intensity shift)
Vortex Future machine environments (artificial gravity and tube navigation complexity)
Vortex Queen multi-phase boss battle (capstone mechanical and narrative challenge)
Atlantis Time Machine final sequence (post-victory tension subversion)

notable_level_patterns.description: Levels focused on navigating open underwater spaces to locate scattered globes or interact with environmental glyphs. Encourages use of sonar echolocation for discovery. Low immediate danger; emphasis on spatial comprehension.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Moray Abyss (globe collection among eel encounters)

notable_level_patterns.description: Pseudo-3D ring-sequence stages requiring precision piloting through moving geometric obstacles. Restart penalty for missed rings creates high-stakes pacing. Distinct mechanical pivot from standard 2D swimming.
Narrative-driven transitions between bright future (flying dolphins, peaceful world) and dark future (Vortex machine, lifeless planet). Environmental theming reinforces timeline stakes.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Lunar Bay and Vortex Future segments

notable_level_patterns.description: Contained spaces designed for multi-phase enemy encounters. Vortex Queen battle serves as final capstone; combines ramming attacks, sonar utility, and spatial awareness.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Vortex New Machine chamber; final Vortex Queen confrontation

notable_level_patterns.description: Levels designed around specific Metasphere animal forms (seagull, jellyfish, shark, fish school, Vortex drone). Each transformation alters movement properties and interaction affordances.
